addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Mechanical sweep with per-locale judgment. The grep is the worklist; the rule
  table is the decision procedure. Several locales resolve to a recorded "no change."
  
  ## Goal
  Eliminate `Tools/` magic-string path literals across executable code, replacing
  each with the established mechanism for its locale (BCG dispatch vars / tinder
  consts for bash; RCG path consts for Rust). Resolve every grep hit — each earns
  a verdict, including no-change for sanctioned forms.
  
  ## Discovery signature
  ```
  rg -n 'Tools/' -g '!target' -g '!*.d'
  ```
  Key property: every compliant form expands a variable (`${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/...`,
  `${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...`, `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...`) and contains NO literal `Tools/`
  substring (case-sensitive grep; `TOOLS_DIR` is uppercase-underscore). So the
  hit-set IS the worklist: each hit is a candidate violation, a sanctioned
  bootstrap, or prose. Triage by locale.
  
  ## Scope (subdirectories)
  `Tools/` (all kits), `rbmm_moorings/` (launchers, compose, Dockerfile), `Study/`,
  `tt/`. Docs are out — see bottom.
  
  ## Per-locale rule
  
  | Locale | Rule |
  |---|---|
  | Dispatched bash CLI / workbench / module | Replace literal with dispatch var: `${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...` (BUK files) or `${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/<kit>/...`. Config path driving a sourcing chain the module owns → tinder const `«PREFIX»_lower_name` instead. Never reconstruct from BASH_SOURCE. (BCG §Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables) |
  | Pre-dispatch launcher body (after BURC sourced) | Use `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...` — the `bul_launch` lines already model this. |
  | Bootstrap membrane — tabtarget `exec` line; launcher's `source "Tools/buk/bul_launcher.sh"` | No change. Runs before any var exists; cwd=repo-root is trampoline-guaranteed; the single sanctioned literal at the membrane. Launchers are generated by `buut_tabtarget` — fix the generator, never the outputs. |
  | Standalone bash not run through dispatch (e.g. uninstaller) | BURD/BURC absent. Resolve own root once via `${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}` into a local and derive the rest, or document a cwd contract. Per-script judgment. |
  | Bash installer/arcanum acting on a target repo (`$target/Tools/...`) | No change where `Tools/...` is the destination contract in the consumer repo — not a self-reference. Verify each: own-location → candidate; target destination → intrinsic. |
  | Rust running-code path | Hoist relative to a `pub const {PREFIX}_SCREAMING: &str` (RCG Identity Rule; exemplar already correct: `VVCC_REGISTRY_PATH`), derive every `root.join(...)` from it. No env-var path in Rust — `root` arrives as a parameter. Const placement/name is mount-time; reuse an existing const home if one fits. |
  | Rust kit-root literals (`Tools/rbk`, `Tools/buk`) | Same const treatment for completeness; lower risk. |
  | Rust comment / docstring | Prose — fix only if the named path is stale. |
  | Rust test fixture / expected-output assertion | The spec of emitted output. Reference the shared const the producer uses, or leave as the expected literal. Lowest priority. |
  | Config without dispatch context — `*compose.yml` bind mounts, `Cargo.toml` path deps, `Dockerfile` | No variable mechanism. Cargo path deps stay static (no change). compose bind-mounts are relative to the compose file = deployment contract (leave unless an orchestrator var is already in play). Dockerfile `Tools/...` is a comment (fix-if-stale). |
  
  ## Out of scope — docs
  `*.md`, `*.adoc`, `Memos/`, root `CLAUDE.md`. The acronym maps and spec cross-refs
  intentionally register `ACRONYM → Tools/...`; keeping them current is the rename
  workflow's job, not this sweep. Flag separately if a rename already left a map stale.
  
  ## Done
  Every `Tools/` grep hit in scope carries a verdict; candidate violations are
  converted to the locale-appropriate mechanism; sanctioned/intrinsic hits are left
  with their rationale; lint and the relevant test tier pass.

14	# jjezs_pace ₢BYAAD

16	## Character
17	Mechanical sweep with per-locale judgment. The grep is the worklist; the rule
18	table is the decision procedure. Several locales resolve to a recorded "no change."
19	
20	## Goal
21	Eliminate `Tools/` magic-string path literals across executable code, replacing
22	each with the established mechanism for its locale (BCG dispatch vars / tinder
23	consts for bash; RCG path consts for Rust). Resolve every grep hit — each earns
24	a verdict, including no-change for sanctioned forms.
25	
26	## Discovery signature
27	```
28	rg -n 'Tools/' -g '!target' -g '!*.d'
29	```
30	Key property: every compliant form expands a variable (`${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/...`,
31	`${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...`, `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...`) and contains NO literal `Tools/`
32	substring (case-sensitive grep; `TOOLS_DIR` is uppercase-underscore). So the
33	hit-set IS the worklist: each hit is a candidate violation, a sanctioned
34	bootstrap, or prose. Triage by locale.
35	
36	## Scope (subdirectories)
37	`Tools/` (all kits), `rbmm_moorings/` (launchers, compose, Dockerfile), `Study/`,
38	`tt/`. Docs are out — see bottom.
39	
40	## Per-locale rule
41	
42	| Locale | Rule |
43	|---|---|
44	| Dispatched bash CLI / workbench / module | Replace literal with dispatch var: `${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...` (BUK files) or `${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/<kit>/...`. Config path driving a sourcing chain the module owns → tinder const `«PREFIX»_lower_name` instead. Never reconstruct from BASH_SOURCE. (BCG §Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables) |
45	| Pre-dispatch launcher body (after BURC sourced) | Use `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...` — the `bul_launch` lines already model this. |
46	| Bootstrap membrane — tabtarget `exec` line; launcher's `source "Tools/buk/bul_launcher.sh"` | No change. Runs before any var exists; cwd=repo-root is trampoline-guaranteed; the single sanctioned literal at the membrane. Launchers are generated by `buut_tabtarget` — fix the generator, never the outputs. |
47	| Standalone bash not run through dispatch (e.g. uninstaller) | BURD/BURC absent. Resolve own root once via `${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}` into a local and derive the rest, or document a cwd contract. Per-script judgment. |
48	| Bash installer/arcanum acting on a target repo (`$target/Tools/...`) | No change where `Tools/...` is the destination contract in the consumer repo — not a self-reference. Verify each: own-location → candidate; target destination → intrinsic. |
49	| Rust running-code path | Hoist relative to a `pub const {PREFIX}_SCREAMING: &str` (RCG Identity Rule; exemplar already correct: `VVCC_REGISTRY_PATH`), derive every `root.join(...)` from it. No env-var path in Rust — `root` arrives as a parameter. Const placement/name is mount-time; reuse an existing const home if one fits. |
50	| Rust kit-root literals (`Tools/rbk`, `Tools/buk`) | Same const treatment for completeness; lower risk. |
51	| Rust comment / docstring | Prose — fix only if the named path is stale. |
52	| Rust test fixture / expected-output assertion | The spec of emitted output. Reference the shared const the producer uses, or leave as the expected literal. Lowest priority. |
53	| Config without dispatch context — `*compose.yml` bind mounts, `Cargo.toml` path deps, `Dockerfile` | No variable mechanism. Cargo path deps stay static (no change). compose bind-mounts are relative to the compose file = deployment contract (leave unless an orchestrator var is already in play). Dockerfile `Tools/...` is a comment (fix-if-stale). |
54	
55	## Out of scope — docs
56	`*.md`, `*.adoc`, `Memos/`, root `CLAUDE.md`. The acronym maps and spec cross-refs
57	intentionally register `ACRONYM → Tools/...`; keeping them current is the rename
58	workflow's job, not this sweep. Flag separately if a rename already left a map stale.
59	
60	## Done
61	Every `Tools/` grep hit in scope carries a verdict; candidate violations are
62	converted to the locale-appropriate mechanism; sanctioned/intrinsic hits are left
63	with their rationale; lint and the relevant test tier pass.
64

# jjezs_paddock ₣BY

# Paddock: rbk-08-credential-repairs

## Context

(Describe the initiative's background and goals)

## References

(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)


# jjezs_pace ₢BYAAD

## Character
Mechanical sweep with per-locale judgment. The grep is the worklist; the rule
table is the decision procedure. Several locales resolve to a recorded "no change."

## Goal
Eliminate `Tools/` magic-string path literals across executable code, replacing
each with the established mechanism for its locale (BCG dispatch vars / tinder
consts for bash; RCG path consts for Rust). Resolve every grep hit — each earns
a verdict, including no-change for sanctioned forms.

## Discovery signature
```
rg -n 'Tools/' -g '!target' -g '!*.d'
```
Key property: every compliant form expands a variable (`${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/...`,
`${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...`, `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...`) and contains NO literal `Tools/`
substring (case-sensitive grep; `TOOLS_DIR` is uppercase-underscore). So the
hit-set IS the worklist: each hit is a candidate violation, a sanctioned
bootstrap, or prose. Triage by locale.

## Scope (subdirectories)
`Tools/` (all kits), `rbmm_moorings/` (launchers, compose, Dockerfile), `Study/`,
`tt/`. Docs are out — see bottom.

## Per-locale rule

| Locale | Rule |
|---|---|
| Dispatched bash CLI / workbench / module | Replace literal with dispatch var: `${BURD_BUK_DIR}/...` (BUK files) or `${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/<kit>/...`. Config path driving a sourcing chain the module owns → tinder const `«PREFIX»_lower_name` instead. Never reconstruct from BASH_SOURCE. (BCG §Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables) |
| Pre-dispatch launcher body (after BURC sourced) | Use `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/...` — the `bul_launch` lines already model this. |
| Bootstrap membrane — tabtarget `exec` line; launcher's `source "Tools/buk/bul_launcher.sh"` | No change. Runs before any var exists; cwd=repo-root is trampoline-guaranteed; the single sanctioned literal at the membrane. Launchers are generated by `buut_tabtarget` — fix the generator, never the outputs. |
| Standalone bash not run through dispatch (e.g. uninstaller) | BURD/BURC absent. Resolve own root once via `${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}` into a local and derive the rest, or document a cwd contract. Per-script judgment. |
| Bash installer/arcanum acting on a target repo (`$target/Tools/...`) | No change where `Tools/...` is the destination contract in the consumer repo — not a self-reference. Verify each: own-location → candidate; target destination → intrinsic. |
| Rust running-code path | Hoist relative to a `pub const {PREFIX}_SCREAMING: &str` (RCG Identity Rule; exemplar already correct: `VVCC_REGISTRY_PATH`), derive every `root.join(...)` from it. No env-var path in Rust — `root` arrives as a parameter. Const placement/name is mount-time; reuse an existing const home if one fits. |
| Rust kit-root literals (`Tools/rbk`, `Tools/buk`) | Same const treatment for completeness; lower risk. |
| Rust comment / docstring | Prose — fix only if the named path is stale. |
| Rust test fixture / expected-output assertion | The spec of emitted output. Reference the shared const the producer uses, or leave as the expected literal. Lowest priority. |
| Config without dispatch context — `*compose.yml` bind mounts, `Cargo.toml` path deps, `Dockerfile` | No variable mechanism. Cargo path deps stay static (no change). compose bind-mounts are relative to the compose file = deployment contract (leave unless an orchestrator var is already in play). Dockerfile `Tools/...` is a comment (fix-if-stale). |

## Out of scope — docs
`*.md`, `*.adoc`, `Memos/`, root `CLAUDE.md`. The acronym maps and spec cross-refs
intentionally register `ACRONYM → Tools/...`; keeping them current is the rename
workflow's job, not this sweep. Flag separately if a rename already left a map stale.

## Done
Every `Tools/` grep hit in scope carries a verdict; candidate violations are
converted to the locale-appropriate mechanism; sanctioned/intrinsic hits are left
with their rationale; lint and the relevant test tier pass.

content.text:
Oriented. Here's the mount briefing for **₣BY / ₢BYAAD**.

## Goal (one sentence)
Eliminate every magic-string `Tools/` path literal in *executable* code, replacing each with its locale's sanctioned mechanism (BCG dispatch vars / tinder consts in bash; RCG path consts in Rust) — and give *every* grep hit a verdict, including documented "no-change" for sanctioned bootstrap/intrinsic forms.

## Heat context
- **₣BY — rbk-08-credential-repairs** [racing], 3/5 done. The done trail is all credential lifecycle (H1 binding-leak, H2 re-key churn, divest read-flap, IAM propagation specs).
- Sincere weather, not a request: this pace is thematically orthogonal to "credential-repairs" — a path-hygiene sweep filed into a credential heat. Your call on whether that's intentional; I'll work it where it sits.
- Paddock is an unfilled template, so the docket is the whole spec — and it's a tight rule table keyed by locale.
- **Name assessment:** `tools-magic-string-sweep` fits its docket exactly. No relabel needed.

## What the worklist actually is (measured just now)
237 raw hits, but docs (24 `.md` + 15 `.adoc`) are out of scope. The **in-scope code worklist is ~34 files**, and it's lopsided:

| Cluster | Files | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Heavy judgment** | `rbtdrf_fast.rs` (29), `voa_arcanum.sh` (18) | ~60% of all code hits. arcanum likely `$target/Tools/...` destination contract → mostly no-change; fast.rs is real const-hoisting. |
| **Generated launchers** | 9× `launcher.*.sh` (1 each) | The `source "Tools/buk/bul_launcher.sh"` bootstrap membrane → **no-change**, but verify the generator `buut_tabtarget.sh` emits it (fix generator, never outputs). |
| **Config** | 3× compose.yml, 1 Cargo.toml, 1 Dockerfile | Almost all no-change (deployment/path-dep contracts; Dockerfile is a comment). |
| **Medium bash/rust** | `cmw_workbench.sh` (6), `rbtdru_cupel.rs` (7), `voff_freshen.rs` (6) | Dispatch-var / const conversions. |
| **Singles** | ~12 files, 1–2 hits | `vvcc_format.rs` holds the cited exemplar `VVCC_REGISTRY_PATH`. |

## Proposed approach (locale-batched, low-risk-first)
1. **Sweep the cheap bulk first** — 9 launchers + 5 config files: confirm each is the sanctioned membrane / deployment contract, record the no-change verdict, and check `buut_tabtarget.sh` is the launcher generator. Clears ~14 files fast.
2. **Bash dispatch conversions** — workbenches/modules to `${BURD_*}` vars or tinder consts; `voa_arcanum.sh` triaged hit-by-hit (own-location → convert; target destination → intrinsic); `vvu_uninstall.sh` as standalone (no BURD/BURC).
3. **Rust const-hoisting** — done per-crate so a shared const lands in one home: the `rbtd` crate (`rbtdrf_fast`/`rbtdru_cupel`/`rbtdrm_manifest`) together, then `vof`, then singles. `vvcc_format.rs` already models it.
4. **Verify** — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh`, plus relevant Rust builds (`tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` for theurge, `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` for VOW) and the `fast` test tier.

## Execution strategy
**Sequential, opus, by me.** The rule table makes most hits mechanical, but two spots carry real breakage risk that wants single-threaded judgment: bash dispatch-var swaps (a wrong var silently breaks a sourcing chain) and Rust const placement (parallel agents in one crate would collide on where the shared const lives). The job is well-bounded — it *would* fan out cleanly across the file clusters if you'd rather trade tokens for wall-clock, but I wouldn't reach for a workflow unasked.

Shall I proceed with step 1?

content.text: Before I run unattended, three decisions that genuinely change what you'll wake up to. Everything else in the docket I can resolve mechanically by reading (e.g., whether `voa_arcanum.sh`'s 18 hits are `$target/Tools/...` destination contracts vs. self-references — the rule table tells me how, I just have to look).

questions.question: How should the ~19 no-change verdicts (bootstrap-membrane launchers, compose/Cargo/Dockerfile config, target-destination contracts) be recorded?

options.label: Report table only
options.description: Leave every no-change file byte-identical; record all verdicts (change + no-change, with rationale) in a consolidated table in my final report. Respects load-bearing-complexity — no noise added to ~19 sanctioned files.

options.label: Inline comments too
options.description: Additionally add a one-line rationale comment at each no-change site. Self-documenting at the site, but touches ~19 otherwise-untouched files and adds comment noise to generated launchers.

questions.question: When the work is complete and lint+tests are green, what end state do you want to find?

options.label: Leave tree dirty
options.description: Stop with the working tree dirty and the pace open. You review the full diff + verdict table, then notch/wrap yourself. Nothing destructive; I never wrap unasked regardless.

options.label: Notch one commit
options.description: Notch a single jjx_record commit on ₢BYAAD when green (additive, safe against loss), pace left open for you to review and wrap. I will NOT wrap.

questions.question: What verification ceiling for the unattended run? (Done = 'lint and the relevant test tier pass'.)

options.label: Lint + builds + fast
options.description: Shellcheck + the affected Rust builds (theurge / VOW / vvc) + the `fast` test suite. No external deps — runs clean unattended. Note: cmw/study/vvu/voa bash get shellcheck + reading only, no runtime coverage.

options.label: Also crucible/complete
options.description: Additionally run heavier tiers. Needs container runtime + GCP credentials — slow and may fail on environment rather than this change, risking a stalled AFK window.

content.text:
Answers logged: **report-table-only** for verdicts, **notch one commit** when green, **also run crucible/complete**, and — noted clearly — **delegate the mechanical clusters to subagents**. That fits: I'll keep the load-bearing judgment (locale triage, shared-const placement, the ambiguous `voa_arcanum`/generator) and hand the bulk conversions to parallel sonnet agents, partitioned by crate/locale so no two agents touch the same file.

First I need the authoritative triage — every in-scope hit in context — so the agent packets are precise. Gathering now.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# VVU Uninstall - Bootstrap script for VVK removal
#
# Thin bootstrap: detect platform, exec vvx_vacate.
# All logic (git checks, commits) is in Rust.
#
# Usage: ./Tools/vvk/vvu_uninstall.sh

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Constants

readonly ZVVU_BURC_RELPATH="rbmm_moorings/burc.env"

######################################################################
# Local BCG-style functions

zvvu_die() {
  echo "vvu_uninstall: error: ${1}" >&2
  exit 1
}

zvvu_step() {
  echo "${1}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Internal functions

zvvu_platform_capture() {
  local z_os
  local z_arch
  local z_platform

  z_os=$(uname -s) || return 1
  z_arch=$(uname -m) || return 1

  case "${z_os}-${z_arch}" in
    Darwin-arm64)   z_platform="darwin-arm64" ;;
    Darwin-x86_64)  z_platform="darwin-x86_64" ;;
    Linux-x86_64)   z_platform="linux-x86_64" ;;
    Linux-aarch64)  z_platform="linux-aarch64" ;;
    *)              return 1 ;;
  esac

  echo "${z_platform}"
}

######################################################################
# Main

zvvu_main() {
  local z_burc_path="${ZVVU_BURC_RELPATH}"

  # Verify we're in a VVK-enabled repo
  test -f "${z_burc_path}" || zvvu_die "Not in a VVK-enabled repo (missing ${z_burc_path})"

  # Get platform and find binary
  local z_platform
  z_platform=$(zvvu_platform_capture) || zvvu_die "Unsupported platform: $(uname -s)-$(uname -m)"

  local z_binary="Tools/vvk/bin/vvx-${z_platform}"
  test -f "${z_binary}" || zvvu_die "VVX binary not found: ${z_binary}"
  test -x "${z_binary}" || zvvu_die "VVX binary not executable: ${z_binary}"

  zvvu_step "Uninstalling VVK..."
  zvvu_step "  Platform: ${z_platform}"

  exec "${z_binary}" vvx_vacate --burc "${z_burc_path}"
}

zvvu_main "$@"

# eof

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
# All rights reserved.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
#
# Study Workbench - Routes study commands
#
# Colophon pattern: study-{id}
# Imprint selects execution mode (e.g., smoke, FULL)

set -euo pipefail

# Get script directory (Study/)
STUDYW_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Source dependencies
source "${STUDYW_SCRIPT_DIR}/../Tools/buk/buc_command.sh"
source "${STUDYW_SCRIPT_DIR}/../Tools/buk/burd_regime.sh"

# Show filename on each displayed line
buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Verify dispatch completed
zburd_kindle

# Verbose output if BURE_VERBOSE is set
studyw_show() {
  test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" != "1" || echo "STUDYWSHOW: $*"
}

# Build a study's Rust binary. Arg: study directory path.
# Returns the binary path via stdout.
studyw_build() {
  local z_study_dir="$1"
  local z_cargo_toml="${z_study_dir}/Cargo.toml"

  test -f "${z_cargo_toml}" || buc_die "No Cargo.toml at ${z_cargo_toml}"

  studyw_show "Building ${z_cargo_toml}"

30	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
31	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
32	
33	# Show filename on each displayed line
34	buc_context "${0##*/}"
35	
36	# Configuration - can be overridden via environment
37	ZCMW_KIT_PATH="${ZCMW_KIT_PATH:-Tools/cmk/README.md}"
38	ZCMW_KIT_DIR="$(dirname "${ZCMW_KIT_PATH}")"
39	
40	# Hardcoded defaults
41	ZCMW_LENSES_DIR="lenses"
42	ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE="OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM"
43	
44	######################################################################
45	# Command Emitters - one function per command file
46	#
47	# Each emits to stdout; caller redirects to file
48	
49	zcmw_emit_prep_pr() {
50	  {
51	    echo "---"
52	    echo "description: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution"
53	    echo "---"
54	    echo ""
55	    echo "You are preparing a PR branch for upstream contribution, stripping proprietary content."
56	    echo ""
57	    echo "**Configuration:**"
58	    echo "- Lenses directory: ${ZCMW_LENSES_DIR}"
59	    echo "- Kit directory: ${ZCMW_KIT_DIR}"
60	    echo "- Kit path: ${ZCMW_KIT_PATH}"
61	    echo "- Upstream remote: ${ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE}"
62	    echo ""
63	    echo "**Execute these steps:**"
64	    echo ""
65	    echo "0. **Request permissions upfront:**"
66	    echo "   - Ask user for permission to execute all git operations needed"
67	    echo "   - Get approval before proceeding"
68	    echo ""
69	    echo "1. **Verify develop is clean and pushed:**"
70	    echo "   - Check \`git status\` on develop branch"
71	    echo "   - Push any uncommitted changes to origin/develop"
72	    echo ""
73	    echo "2. **Sync main with upstream:**"
74	    echo "   - \`git checkout main\`"
75	    echo "   - \`git fetch ${ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE}\`"
76	    echo "   - \`git pull ${ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE} main\`"
77	    echo "   - If pull fails, **ABORT** and ask user to resolve"
78	    echo "   - \`git push origin main\`"
79	    echo ""
80	    echo "3. **Determine candidate branch (ask user):**"
81	    echo "   - List existing local candidate branches: \`git branch -a | grep candidate-\`"
82	    echo "   - Find highest batch number (NNN) and revision (R) from local branches"
83	    echo "   - Ask user using AskUserQuestion tool:"
84	    echo "     - **New delivery**: Previous PR was merged → create candidate-{N+1}-1"
85	    echo "     - **Reissue/fix**: Previous PR pending or rejected → create candidate-{N}-{R+1}"
86	    echo "   - If no prior candidates exist, default to candidate-001-1"
87	    echo "   - Confirm the branch name with user before proceeding"
88	    echo ""
89	    echo "4. **Create candidate branch:**"
90	    echo "   - \`git checkout -b candidate-NNN-R main\`"
91	    echo ""
92	    echo "5. **Squash merge develop:**"
93	    echo "   - Show commits: \`git log main..develop --oneline\`"
94	    echo "   - Execute: \`git merge --squash develop\`"
95	    echo ""
96	    echo "6. **Strip proprietary content:**"
97	    echo "   - \`git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch .claude/\`"
98	    echo "   - \`git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch ${ZCMW_LENSES_DIR}/\`"
99	    echo "   - Verify removal with \`git ls-files\`"
100	    echo ""
101	    echo "7. **Generate commit:**"
102	    echo "   - Analyze changes for consolidated message"
103	    echo "   - Filter out commits that only touch stripped files"
104	    echo "   - Create commit (no attribution footer)"
105	    echo ""
106	    echo "8. **Final review:**"
107	    echo "   - Show \`git log -1 --stat\`"
108	    echo "   - Display push instructions"
109	    echo "   - **STOP** - user reviews and pushes manually"
110	    echo ""
111	    echo "**Important:**"
112	    echo "- Be methodical, show output at each step"
113	    echo "- Stop immediately on errors"
114	    echo "- User maintains control over final push"
115	  }
116	}
117	
118	## RETIRED: zcmw_emit_claudemd_section
119	## CMK context is now maintained as Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md
120	## and included via @-directive in CLAUDE.md. No patching needed.
121	
122	######################################################################
123	# Main Commands
124	
125	cmw_install() {
126	  buc_step "Clearing previous installation"
127	  cmw_uninstall
128	
129	  buc_step "Creating directory structure"
130	  mkdir -p ".claude/commands"
131	  mkdir -p "${ZCMW_LENSES_DIR}"
132	
133	  buc_step "Emitting command files"

file.content:
# Source dependencies
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"

# Show filename on each displayed line
buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Configuration - can be overridden via environment
ZCMW_KIT_PATH="${ZCMW_KIT_PATH:-Tools/cmk/README.md}"
ZCMW_KIT_DIR="$(dirname "${ZCMW_KIT_PATH}")"

# Hardcoded defaults
ZCMW_LENSES_DIR="lenses"
ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE="OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM"

######################################################################
# Command Emitters - one function per command file
#
# Each emits to stdout; caller redirects to file

zcmw_emit_prep_pr() {
  {
    echo "---"
    echo "description: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution"
    echo "---"
    echo ""
    echo "You are preparing a PR branch for upstream contribution, stripping proprietary content."
    echo ""
    echo "**Configuration:**"
    echo "- Lenses directory: ${ZCMW_LENSES_DIR}"
    echo "- Kit directory: ${ZCMW_KIT_DIR}"
    echo "- Kit path: ${ZCMW_KIT_PATH}"
    echo "- Upstream remote: ${ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE}"
    echo ""
    echo "**Execute these steps:**"
    echo ""
    echo "0. **Request permissions upfront:**"
    echo "   - Ask user for permission to execute all git operations needed"
    echo "   - Get approval before proceeding"
    echo ""
    echo "1. **Verify develop is clean and pushed:**"
    echo "   - Check \`git status\` on develop branch"
    echo "   - Push any uncommitted changes to origin/develop"
    echo ""
    echo "2. **Sync main with upstream:**"
    echo "   - \`git checkout main\`"
    echo "   - \`git fetch ${ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE}\`"
    echo "   - \`git pull ${ZCMW_UPSTREAM_REMOTE} main\`"
    echo "   - If pull fails, **ABORT** and ask user to resolve"
    echo "   - \`git push origin main\`"
    echo ""
    echo "3. **Determine candidate branch (ask user):**"
    echo "   - List existing local candidate branches: \`git branch -a | grep candidate-\`"
    echo "   - Find highest batch number (NNN) and revision (R) from local branches"
    echo "   - Ask user using AskUserQuestion tool:"
    echo "     - **New delivery**: Previous PR was merged → create candidate-{N+1}-1"
    echo "     - **Reissue/fix**: Previous PR pending or rejected → create candidate-{N}-{R+1}"
    echo "   - If no prior candidates exist, default to candidate-001-1"
    echo "   - Confirm the branch name with user before proceeding"
    echo ""
    echo "4. **Create candidate branch:**"
    echo "   - \`git checkout -b candidate-NNN-R main\`"
    echo ""
    echo "5. **Squash merge develop:**"
    echo "   - Show commits: \`git log main..develop --oneline\`"
    echo "   - Execute: \`git merge --squash develop\`"
    echo ""
    echo "6. **Strip proprietary content:**"
    echo "   - \`git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch .claude/\`"
    echo "   - \`git rm -rf --ignore-unmatch ${ZCMW_LENSES_DIR}/\`"
    echo "   - Verify removal with \`git ls-files\`"
    echo ""
    echo "7. **Generate commit:**"
    echo "   - Analyze changes for consolidated message"
    echo "   - Filter out commits that only touch stripped files"
    echo "   - Create commit (no attribution footer)"
    echo ""
    echo "8. **Final review:**"
    echo "   - Show \`git log -1 --stat\`"
    echo "   - Display push instructions"
    echo "   - **STOP** - user reviews and pushes manually"
    echo ""
    echo "**Important:**"
    echo "- Be methodical, show output at each step"
    echo "- Stop immediately on errors"
    echo "- User maintains control over final push"
  }
}

## RETIRED: zcmw_emit_claudemd_section
## CMK context is now maintained as Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md
## and included via @-directive in CLAUDE.md. No patching needed.

######################################################################
# Main Commands

cmw_install() {
  buc_step "Clearing previous installation"
  cmw_uninstall

  buc_step "Creating directory structure"
  mkdir -p ".claude/commands"
  mkdir -p "${ZCMW_LENSES_DIR}"

  buc_step "Emitting command files"
  zcmw_emit_prep_pr     > ".claude/commands/cma-prep-pr.md"

  buc_step "Verifying CLAUDE.md include directive"
  if ! grep -q '@Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md' CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_warn "CLAUDE.md does not contain '@Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md' include directive"
    buc_warn "Add this line to CLAUDE.md: @Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md"
  fi

  buc_success "Concept Model Kit installed"
  echo ""
  echo "Restart Claude Code for new commands to become available."
}

cmw_uninstall() {
  buc_step "Removing command files"
  rm -f .claude/commands/cma-*.md

  buc_success "Concept Model Kit uninstalled (context file preserved in kit directory)"
}

A repo-relative literal is the correct emitted-doc form; injecting `${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}` would bake a machine-absolute path into a committed artifact. Good thing I read it.
2. **`cmw_workbench.sh:119/137/138/156`** reference `@Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md` — but the root `CLAUDE.md` now includes `@Tools/cmk/claude-cmk-core.md`. That file was **renamed**, leaving the install-verification grep (line 137) dead (warns on every correct install). This is rename-staleness, which the docket explicitly scopes *out* ("Flag separately"). I'll **flag, not fix**.
3. **`vvu_uninstall.sh:80`** is a true standalone-bootstrap candidate — the file already establishes a cwd=repo-root contract via `ZVVU_BURC_RELPATH`, so I'll hoist line 80 to a sibling const (the locale's own established pattern). **`study_workbench.sh`** I need to verify the launch chain before deciding.

The Rust const scheme for `rbtdrf_fast.rs` is fully designed (26 running-code sites → 5 hoisted consts; 3 emitted-bash/docstring lines left untouched). Dispatching that mechanical application to an agent, plus an adversarial auditor to challenge my ~76 no-change verdicts, while I verify the study launch chain in parallel.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
# All rights reserved.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
#
# Study Workbench - Routes study commands
#
# Colophon pattern: study-{id}
# Imprint selects execution mode (e.g., smoke, FULL)

set -euo pipefail

# Get script directory (Study/)
STUDYW_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Source dependencies
source "${STUDYW_SCRIPT_DIR}/../Tools/buk/buc_command.sh"
source "${STUDYW_SCRIPT_DIR}/../Tools/buk/burd_regime.sh"

# Show filename on each displayed line
buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Verify dispatch completed
zburd_kindle

# Verbose output if BURE_VERBOSE is set
studyw_show() {
  test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" != "1" || echo "STUDYWSHOW: $*"
}

# Build a study's Rust binary. Arg: study directory path.
# Returns the binary path via stdout.
studyw_build() {
  local z_study_dir="$1"
  local z_cargo_toml="${z_study_dir}/Cargo.toml"

  test -f "${z_cargo_toml}" || buc_die "No Cargo.toml at ${z_cargo_toml}"

  studyw_show "Building ${z_cargo_toml}"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${z_cargo_toml}" >&2

  # Extract binary name from Cargo.toml [[bin]] section
  local z_bin_name
  z_bin_name=$(grep '^name' "${z_cargo_toml}" | head -1 | sed 's/.*= *"\(.*\)"/\1/')
  echo "${z_study_dir}/target/debug/${z_bin_name}"
}

# Route based on colophon and imprint
studyw_route() {
  local z_command="$1"
  shift

  studyw_show "Routing command: ${z_command} with args: $*"

  zburd_sentinel

  local z_imprint="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"

  studyw_show "Imprint: '${z_imprint}'"

  case "${z_command}" in

    study-mpt)
      local z_study_dir="${STUDYW_SCRIPT_DIR}/study-model-prompt-tuning"
      local z_binary
      z_binary=$(studyw_build "${z_study_dir}")

      case "${z_imprint}" in
        smoke)    exec "${z_binary}" smoke ;;
        FULL)     exec "${z_binary}" run "$@" ;;
        api-FULL) exec "${z_binary}" api-run "$@" ;;
        *)        exec "${z_binary}" "$@"  ;;
      esac
      ;;

    # Add future studies here:
    # study-xyz)
    #   ...
    #   ;;

    *)  buc_die "Unknown study command: ${z_command}" ;;
  esac
}

studyw_main() {
  local z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"

  studyw_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

studyw_main "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# VVU Uninstall - Bootstrap script for VVK removal
#
# Thin bootstrap: detect platform, exec vvx_vacate.
# All logic (git checks, commits) is in Rust.
#
# Usage: ./Tools/vvk/vvu_uninstall.sh

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Constants

readonly ZVVU_BURC_RELPATH="rbmm_moorings/burc.env"

######################################################################
# Local BCG-style functions

zvvu_die() {
  echo "vvu_uninstall: error: ${1}" >&2
  exit 1
}

zvvu_step() {
  echo "${1}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Internal functions

zvvu_platform_capture() {
  local z_os
  local z_arch
  local z_platform

  z_os=$(uname -s) || return 1
  z_arch=$(uname -m) || return 1

  case "${z_os}-${z_arch}" in
    Darwin-arm64)   z_platform="darwin-arm64" ;;
    Darwin-x86_64)  z_platform="darwin-x86_64" ;;
    Linux-x86_64)   z_platform="linux-x86_64" ;;
    Linux-aarch64)  z_platform="linux-aarch64" ;;
    *)              return 1 ;;
  esac

  echo "${z_platform}"
}

######################################################################
# Main

zvvu_main() {
  local z_burc_path="${ZVVU_BURC_RELPATH}"

  # Verify we're in a VVK-enabled repo
  test -f "${z_burc_path}" || zvvu_die "Not in a VVK-enabled repo (missing ${z_burc_path})"

  # Get platform and find binary
  local z_platform
  z_platform=$(zvvu_platform_capture) || zvvu_die "Unsupported platform: $(uname -s)-$(uname -m)"

  local z_binary="Tools/vvk/bin/vvx-${z_platform}"
  test -f "${z_binary}" || zvvu_die "VVX binary not found: ${z_binary}"
  test -x "${z_binary}" || zvvu_die "VVX binary not executable: ${z_binary}"

  zvvu_step "Uninstalling VVK..."
  zvvu_step "  Platform: ${z_platform}"

  exec "${z_binary}" vvx_vacate --burc "${z_burc_path}"
}

zvvu_main "$@"

# eof

`crucible` is the container-runtime tier — it needs a live Docker daemon (which you have, 29.5.3). It's **117 cases**: the `fast` tier plus three container fixtures.

| Component | Cases | What it exercises |
|---|---|---|
| **fast** (subset) | 75 | enrollment-validation (47), regime-validation (21), regime-smoke (7) — pure config/validation, no containers |
| **tadmor-security** | 34 | The adversarial containment suite — charges the `tadmor` crucible (sentry + bottle) and runs ifrit attack sorties to prove the sentry contains the bottle (egress jailing, iptables/dnsmasq enclave, the conntrack/off-path-reply checks this heat's neighbor ₣BB has been hardening) |
| **srjcl-jupyter** | 3 | Jupyter-vessel nameplate — container charges and serves |
| **pluml-diagram** | 5 | PlantUML-vessel nameplate — diagram rendering in-container |

So it's the tier that verifies **real container behavior and the security membrane** end-to-end, versus `fast` (validation logic only) or the credential suites like dogfight/skirmish (GCP IAM lifecycle).

Relevance to this sweep: crucible re-runs the `fast` fixtures — which is exactly where my `rbtdrf_fast.rs` const changes live — through a full container charge, plus confirms nothing in the sentry/bottle path regressed. My bash edits (study/vvu) aren't touched by any suite, so shellcheck remains their gate.

One caveat I'll re-flag when it runs: tadmor-security needs the `tadmor` sentry+bottle **vessel images built locally**. If they aren't present, the charge fails on missing images — an environment-setup gap, not a regression. I'll report that distinctly if it surfaces.

Still waiting on dogfight; skirmish → crucible follow automatically.

# RBGJAM Step 01: Discover platforms from image manifest in GAR
# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud
# Substitutions (GCB anchors — automapSubstitutions provides as env vars):
#   ${_RBGA_GAR_HOST} ${_RBGA_GAR_PATH} ${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}
#   ${_RBGA_HALLMARK} ${_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE}
#   ${_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE} ${_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS}
#
# Queries the image manifest to discover what platforms are present.
# Handles OCI image index (multi-platform) vs single OCI/Docker manifest.
# Writes platform info to /workspace for subsequent steps:
#   platforms.txt          - comma-separated platform list (linux/amd64,linux/arm64)
#   platform_suffixes.txt  - comma-separated suffix list (-amd64,-arm64)
#   platform_count.txt     - number of platforms
#   platform_digests.txt   - suffix-to-digest mapping (one line per platform: -amd64 sha256:...)

import json
import os
import sys
import tarfile
import urllib.error
import urllib.request
from io import BytesIO

ACCEPT_ALL = ",".join([
    "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
    "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json",
    "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
    "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
])

DIAGS_ACCEPT = ",".join([
    "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
    "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
])


def die(msg):
    print(msg, file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)


def require_env(name):
    val = os.environ.get(name, "")
    if not val:
        die(f"{name} missing")
    return val


def get_hallmark():
    """Get hallmark from env var (standalone about) or file (combined conjure)."""
    val = os.environ.get("_RBGA_HALLMARK", "")
    if val:
        return val
    try:
        with open(".hallmark") as f:
            return f.read().strip()
    except FileNotFoundError:
        die("_RBGA_HALLMARK not set and .hallmark file not found")


METADATA_TOKEN_URL = (
    "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/"
    "instance/service-accounts/default/token"
)


def metadata_token():
    """Fetch OAuth2 access token from GCE metadata server."""
    req = urllib.request.Request(METADATA_TOKEN_URL, headers={"Metadata-Flavor": "Google"})
    resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req)
    return json.loads(resp.read())["access_token"]


def gar_fetch(url, token, accept, method="GET"):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Accept": accept}
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers, method=method)
    return urllib.request.urlopen(req)


def gar_json(url, token, accept):
    resp = gar_fetch(url, token, accept)
    return json.loads(resp.read())


def main():
    gar_host        = require_env("_RBGA_GAR_HOST")
    gar_path        = require_env("_RBGA_GAR_PATH")
    hallmarks_root  = require_env("_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT")
    image_basename  = require_env("_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE")
    diags_basename  = require_env("_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS")
    hallmark        = get_hallmark()
    _               = require_env("_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE")

    # New layout: image package = <HALLMARKS_ROOT>/<HALLMARK>/<image-basename>, tag = <HALLMARK>
    image_package = f"{hallmarks_root}/{hallmark}/{image_basename}"
    diags_package = f"{hallmarks_root}/{hallmark}/{diags_basename}"
    image_registry_base = f"https://{gar_host}/v2/{gar_path}/{image_package}"
    diags_registry_base = f"https://{gar_host}/v2/{gar_path}/{diags_package}"

    print("Fetching OAuth2 token via metadata server")
    token = metadata_token()

    print(f"=== Discovering platforms for {image_package}:{hallmark} ===")

    try:
        manifest = gar_json(f"{image_registry_base}/manifests/{hallmark}", token, ACCEPT_ALL)
    except Exception as e:
        die(f"ned to fetch manifest for {image_package}:{hallmark}: {e}")

    media_type = manifest.get("mediaType", "")
    print(f"Manifest media type: {media_type}")

    is_index = "manifest.list" in media_type or "image.index" in media_type

    if is_index:
        _discover_index(manifest)
    else:
        _discover_single(manifest, image_registry_base, token, hallmark)

    with open("platforms.txt") as f:
        print(f"Platforms: {f.read()}")
    with open("platform_suffixes.txt") as f:
        print(f"Suffixes: {f.read()}")
    with open("platform_count.txt") as f:
        print(f"Count: {f.read()}")
    print("=== Platform discovery complete ===")

    _extract_diags(diags_registry_base, token, hallmark)


def _normalize_variant(arch, variant):
    """Strip default-variant pairs to match containerd/buildkit normalization.

    Transcribes the table in containerd's platforms/defaults.go Normalize():
        arm64 + v8  → arm64 (v8 was the original, now-implicit arm64 default)
        amd64 + v1  → amd64 (v1 is baseline x86_64; v2/v3/v4 are kept)

    Without this, our pipeline disagrees with itself across reads. A config
    blob (raw, never normalized — e.g. a Mac M-series image carries
    architecture=arm64, variant=v8) produces suffix=-arm64v8, while step 04
    feeds the same platform string to buildx, which normalizes again and
    exposes ${TARGETARCH}${TARGETVARIANT} as plain "arm64" — yielding a
    sbom-arm64.json:not-found COPY nure. Manifest-list entries written by
    buildx are already normalized, so _discover_index sees no v8; this helper
    is symmetric defense in case an upstream registry serves a non-normalized
    list.
    """
    if arch == "arm64" and variant == "v8":
        return ""
    if arch == "amd64" and variant == "v1":
        return ""
    return variant


def _discover_index(manifest):
    all_entries = manifest.get("manifests", [])
    print(f"Multi-platform manifest detected ({len(all_entries)} entries in index)")

    # Filter out attestation manifests (platform unknown/unknown)
    # BuildKit stores SLSA provenance and SBOM attestations as manifest entries
    # with platform unknown/unknown to prevent runtimes from pulling them as images.
    entries = []
    attestation_count = 0
    for m in all_entries:
        plat = m.get("platform")
        if not plat:
            continue
        os_name = plat.get("os", "")
        arch = plat.get("architecture", "")
        if os_name == "unknown" and arch == "unknown":
            attestation_count += 1
            continue
        variant = _normalize_variant(arch, plat.get("variant", ""))
        platform_str = f"{os_name}/{arch}"
        suffix = f"-{arch}"
        if variant:
            platform_str += f"/{variant}"
            suffix += variant
        entries.append((platform_str, m["digest"], suffix))

    if attestation_count > 0:
        print(f"Filtered {attestation_count} attestation manifest(s) (platform unknown/unknown)")

    if not entries:
        die("No runnable platforms found after filtering attestation manifests")

    with open("platforms.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(",".join(e[0] for e in entries))
    with open("platform_suffixes.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(",".join(e[2] for e in entries))
    with open("platform_count.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(str(len(entries)))
    with open("platform_digests.txt", "w") as f:
        for _, digest, suffix in entries:
            f.write(f"{suffix} {digest}\n")


def _discover_single(manifest, image_registry_base, token, hallmark):
    print("Single manifest detected")

    config_digest = manifest.get("config", {}).get("digest", "")
    if not config_digest:
        die("No config digest in manifest")

    try:
        config = gar_json(f"{image_registry_base}/blobs/{config_digest}", token, ACCEPT_ALL)
    except Exception as e:
        die(f"ned to fetch config blob: {e}")

    os_name = config.get("os", "")
    arch    = config.get("architecture", "")
    variant = _normalize_variant(arch, config.get("variant", ""))

    if variant:
        platform = f"{os_name}/{arch}/{variant}"
        suffix = f"-{arch}{variant}"
    else:
        platform = f"{os_name}/{arch}"
        suffix = f"-{arch}"

    # Get manifest digest from HEAD response
    try:
        resp = gar_fetch(f"{image_registry_base}/manifests/{hallmark}", token, ACCEPT_ALL, method="HEAD")
        manifest_digest = resp.headers.get("Docker-Content-Digest", "")
    except Exception as e:
        die(f"ned HEAD for manifest digest: {e}")
    if not manifest_digest:
        die("No Docker-Content-Digest in HEAD response")

    with open("platforms.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(platform)
    with open("platform_suffixes.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(suffix)
    with open("platform_count.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write("1")
    with open("platform_digests.txt", "w") as f:
        f.write(f"{suffix} {manifest_digest}\n")


def _extract_diags(diags_registry_base, token, hallmark):
    print()
    print(f"=== Checking for diags artifact at {diags_registry_base}:{hallmark} ===")

    try:
        diags_manifest = gar_json(
            f"{diags_registry_base}/manifests/{hallmark}", token, DIAGS_ACCEPT,
        )
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        print(f"No diags artifact found (HTTP {e.code}) — expected for bind/graft")
        print("=== diags check complete ===")
        return
    except Exception:

                if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):
                    tar.extractall(filter="data")
                else:
                    tar.extractall()

content.text:
Now I can give you the exact boundary. Here's skirmish's full fixture order (from `rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2514`) with the cloud-dependent ones marked:

| # | Fixture | Cloud? | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | enrollment-validation (47) | local | ✅ done |
| 2 | **canonical-invest** (IAM credential set) | ☁️ **GCP IAM** | ▶ in progress |
| 3 | **onboarding-sequence** | ☁️ **Cloud Build ordain** (+ local crucible charges) | pending |
| 4 | regime-validation (21) | local | pending |
| 5 | regime-smoke (7) | local | pending |
| 6 | dockerfile-hygiene | local | pending |
| 7 | cupel (BCG lint) | local | pending |
| 8–11 | tadmor / moriah / srjcl / pluml | local Docker | pending |

**The cloud steps are #2 and #3.** Once **onboarding-sequence (#3) passes, you're past all GCP cloud work** — everything from #4 on is local validation + local-Docker crucibles (no GCP spend, no auth dependency). #3 is the long pole: it does the real Cloud Build ordain (~5–6 min, like dogfight's 330s) plus local image builds.

**Watch-for marker:** the log transitioning to `rbtdrf_rv_*` cases (regime-validation, fixture #4) — that's the first line *after* the cloud is fully behind it. Rough estimate: ~10 min from now (canonical-invest finishing, then the ordain).

Honest note on mechanism: the harness only wakes me on *full suite* completion, not at this mid-run boundary. So I won't auto-catch the #3→#4 transition — say "peek" again in ~10 min and I'll confirm you're clear of the cloud, or I'll fold it into the final report when the whole suite finishes. A nice side effect: #3 builds the tadmor/moriah images, so the subsequent standalone `crucible` run won't hit the missing-image gap I flagged earlier.

content.text:
Confirmed, and you're right to push on it — my "@-directive" phrasing was sloppy. Nothing in the bash script activates an `@`-include. Here's what's actually going on:

**What line 137 really does.** `cmw_install` is the CMK *installer*. After it emits the slash-command files, it verifies that the **project's `CLAUDE.md`** contains the include line that will make *Claude* (not bash) load the CMK context. It does that with a plain text grep:

```bash
if ! grep -q '@Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md' CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null; then
  buc_warn "CLAUDE.md does not contain '@Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md' include directive"
  buc_warn "Add this line to CLAUDE.md: @Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md"
fi
```

The `@` is just the **first character of the literal string being searched for**. The `@`-include mechanism matters in `CLAUDE.md` (where Claude reads it), not in the script. The script is only asking "does CLAUDE.md contain that expected directive line?" — string matching, exactly as you'd expect. So your instinct is correct: the `@` does nothing *to* the bash script.

**Where the bug is.** The needle is the **old filename**. The verification confirms it (from the run just now):

- File on disk: `claude-cmk-core.md` — `cmk-claude-context.md` **does not exist** (name was flipped: `cmk-claude-context` → `claude-cmk-core`).
- `CLAUDE.md:246` actually includes `@Tools/cmk/claude-cmk-core.md`.
- `cmw_install:137` greps for `@Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md`.

So on a **correctly** installed repo, the grep finds nothing → `! grep` is true → it prints "CLAUDE.md does not contain ... include directive" and tells the user to add `@Tools/cmk/cmk-claude-context.md` — a file that doesn't exist. The check is effectively inverted: it false-alarms on a healthy install and hands out stale remediation. It can never warn *correctly*.

**Severity — low, and that's why it's not urgent.** It's cosmetic: the install still succeeds; this is just a nag + wrong filename in the advice. It only fires when someone runs `cmw_install` into a target repo (CMK is the distributable kit). In *this* repo nobody re-runs it. The fix is trivial — swap the old name for `claude-cmk-core.md` on lines 137/138/139 and the two comments (119, 156).

**Why the sweep left it alone.** The sweep converts path literals the *code resolves on the filesystem* into dispatch vars. This string is neither — it's a search needle for *another file's contents*, so the mechanism rule is correctly "no change." The stale **filename** is a rename-follow-through miss, which the docket explicitly assigns to the rename workflow, not this sweep. That's the line I drew when I flagged-but-didn't-fix it.

Want me to fix the five lines as a small standalone pace, or leave it for the rename pass?

Mounted ₣BX. Here's the honest assessment — and it's a **partial match, not a full one.** I want to be straight about the gap rather than tell you what we'd both like to hear.

## ₢BXAAH — milestone-skirmish-gate (the goal)
Prove the capture-machinery-unification refactor behavior-preserving against the live system. Heavyweight, operator-driven. Its **Done** has three parts: *(a)* full skirmish green, *(b)* **service lode-lifecycle** green, *(c)* artifacts/provenance verdicts identical to pre-refactor (provenance-insensitivity-to-restructuring confirmed).

## What this chat actually covered, leg by leg

| Done leg | Covered? | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **(a) Full skirmish green** | ✅ Yes | skirmish 254/254 (1 skip), on a HEAD that contains all 9 BX refactor commits — so it's valid evidence the unified onboarding→build→crucible chain is behavior-preserving |
| **(b) service lode-lifecycle green** | ❌ **No** | The `lode-lifecycle` fixture (ensconce busybox→Lode → enumerate → inspect vouch envelope → banish → confirm gone) is the *single most on-point test of this heat*. It lives **only** in `service`/`complete` (defs at lines 2440/2475). I never ran `service`. **None** of what we ran — dogfight, skirmish, crucible — includes it (verified: skirmish & crucible fixture lists have no `LODE_LIFECYCLE`). |
| **(c) provenance identical to pre-refactor** | ⚠️ Partial | Skirmish's ordain/vouch assertions passing shows provenance preserved for the **conjure/build** path. But the **Lode capture** provenance — the vouch envelope that rides into the captured member, asserted by lode-lifecycle's inspect step — is exactly what wasn't exercised. The heart of the refactor is the Lode spine; that's the provenance leg still unproven. |

## Verdict
**This chat does not fully discharge ₢BXAAH.** It nails the skirmish leg cleanly, but leaves the **lode-lifecycle** leg — the part most directly testing the capture-machinery the heat unified — untouched.

## One nuance worth flagging
Already-landed pace ₢BXAAG's commit says behavior-preservation was *"confirmed at the heat's milestone skirmish gate (full skirmish + service)"* — which implies a `service` run may have happened in a **prior session**. I can't verify that from this chat. If it did, AAH may be closable by recording it; if not, the gate genuinely still needs the service leg.

## The good news / cheapest path to close it
The environment is warm right now: you reauthenticated, GCP auth is proven (dogfight/skirmish credential cases all green), and a standing canonical depot exists (canonical-invest passed) — both of the cinched preconditions are satisfied this session. So running **`tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.service.sh`** now (or, narrowly, just the `lode-lifecycle` fixture via `tt/rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh lode-lifecycle`) would close leg (b) and supply the Lode-path provenance for leg (c).

How do you want to proceed — run full `service`, run just the `lode-lifecycle` fixture, or do you recall already running service in a prior session (in which case I'd help you record/wrap AAH instead)?

48	
49	When generating heredocs for stdin content, the delimiter must not appear alone on any line within the content.
50	
51	- **Check content first**: If content includes `EOF` (e.g., code examples showing heredoc patterns), use a different delimiter
52	- **Safe alternatives**: `SPEC`, `CONTENT`, `DOC`, `PACESPEC`, `SLASHCMD`

56	When working with .adoc files using MCM patterns:
57	- **Linked Term**: Concept with three parts:
58	  - Attribute reference: `:prefix_snake_case:` (mapping section)
59	  - Replacement text: `<<anchor,Display Text>>` (what readers see)

104	
105	When asked to "mint" names, apply these rules. Full study: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`
106	
107	### Two Universes
108	
109	**Primary Universe** — ANY persistent identifier, regardless of where it lives. This includes the obvious (code, docs, functions, variables, directories) AND the easy-to-miss: git refs, slash commands, environment variables, paths in target repos, configuration keys. **If it's a name that persists, it's in scope.** Prefixes must be globally unique and respect terminal exclusivity.
110	
111	**Tabtarget Universe** — launchers in `tt/`. These are *colophons* referencing the primary universe. `rbw-` points to the `rbw` workbench; it doesn't consume new prefix space.
112	
113	### Core Rules
114	
115	**Rule 1 - Project Prefix**: Names start with 2-4 char project ID:
116	`rb` (Recipe Bottle), `gad` (GAD), `bu` (BUK), `jj` (Job Jockey), `pb` (Paneboard), `mcm`/`axl` (CMK), `crg`, `wrs`, `hm` (HMK), `lmci`, `vsl` (VSLK)
117	
118	**Rule 2 - Terminal Exclusivity**: A prefix either IS a name or HAS children, never both.
119	- `rbg` has children (`rbga`, `rbgb`...) → `rbg` cannot name a thing
120	- `rbi` names Image module → `rbia`, `rbib` forbidden
121

content.text:
Verified by reading exact bytes — the `"n"` was a rendering artifact. The real constant (rbcc_Constants.sh:37) is:

```bash
readonly RBCC_tabtarget_context_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md"
```

with `RBCC_KIT_DIR` = `${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}` (= `Tools/rbk`), resolving to `Tools/rbk/claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md` — which exists. Correct, and incidentally clean (derived from KIT_DIR, no `Tools/` literal). (Also: `rbcc_` and `rbgc_` are distinct coexisting files — Claude-meta consts vs Google consts — not a collision.)

## Critical review verdict: the fix is correct *and* complete

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| **The `@` fix itself** | ✅ cmw:137 greps `@Tools/cmk/claude-cmk-core.md`, exactly matching CLAUDE.md:246's real include. All 5 sites (137/138/139 + comments 119/156) updated. The latent false-warning bug is closed. |
| **No dangling OLD references** | ✅ Zero hits for any of the 9 pre-rename names across the whole repo (outside Memos/). The reference side is fully retargeted — the staleness wasn't relocated. |
| **All NEW targets resolve** | ✅ All 11 root CLAUDE.md `@`-includes point to files that exist; the live `RBCC_tabtarget_context_file` constant resolves to a real file. |
| **Other kits' installers** | ✅ The repo-wide old-name grep would have caught an analogous stale grep in `voa_arcanum`/`apcw`/etc. — none exists. So this latent-bug class is closed kit-wide, not just in cmw. |

So the parallel chat didn't just patch my one flag — it did the clean version: rename + exhaustive retarget, with no broken edge in either direction. My original concern is **resolved**; no separate pace needed for it.

## Honest scope limits of this review
- I **excluded `Memos/`** — historical record; old names there are correct (a memo documenting past state shouldn't be rewritten). Worth confirming that's your intent, but it's the right default.
- I did **not** verify the **consumer-repo** `@`-includes (`CLAUDE.consumer.md`) resolve — those are destination contracts pointing into a *consumer's* tree, not checkable from here. The commit claims it fixed the long-broken `buk-claude-context.md → claude-buk-core.md` consumer ref; I'm taking that on the commit's word, not independent verification.
- I did **not** re-run shellcheck on the pulled changes (commit asserts 200 files clean; the cmw edit is trivial string swaps).

Net: I'd trust this. The one thing I can't close from this station is consumer-repo resolution — if you want certainty there, it'd take an actual CMK/BUK install into a scratch consumer repo.
